generation.final_prompt:
Rendering style:
Create an authored editorial illustration for a serious technology newspaper.

Direction:
- authored editorial print language
- deliberate simplified shapes with clear focal hierarchy
- restrained limited palette
- subtle physical print texture
- controlled abstraction and strong negative space
- non-photorealistic but factually grounded representation
- newspaper and social-card readability

Fixed factual and safety restrictions:
- Do not render visible text, captions, headlines, or newspaper typography.
- Do not render logos or trademarks.
- Do not fabricate visible factual evidence.
- Do not render interfaces or screenshots.
- Do not imitate a named or living artist.

Profile-specific exclusions:
- glossy CGI
- luxury product render
- generic futuristic concept art
- cyberpunk or neon
- glowing circuit-board decoration
- floating particles
- heroic centered hardware
- server rack treated as monument or skyscraper
- generic server room
- advertising composition
- airbrushed gradients
- synthetic chrome
- excessive pseudo-technical detail
- malformed or visible text
- labels
- diagrams with words

Exact source facts:
Title: WHAT SYSTEMS THINKING LOOKS LIKE FOR PM'ING AI PRODUCTS
Summary: AI products must be cultivated as evolving systems rather than designed as isolated features. Product teams can create lasting differentiation by building reusable interfaces, shared contracts, and feedback loops that help agents improve over time.

Editorial idea:
AI products become durable systems when reusable interfaces, shared contracts, and feedback loops help agents improve over time.

Central subject:
An evolving AI product system

Visual relationship:
Reusable product foundations support a cycle of use, feedback, and improvement rather than a single isolated feature.

Composition:
Show a cultivated path of connected stages, with a stable shared foundation beneath it and feedback returning from later stages to earlier ones.

Permitted literal elements:
Interlocking pathways
A visible foundation beneath the pathways
Small markers showing repeated user feedback and system improvement

Abstraction level:
medium

Story-specific forbidden elements:
Racks
Chips
Laptops
Robots
Clouds
Shields
Arrows
Charts
Skyscrapers
Chess
Handshakes
Split-screen conflict
Rendered text
Logos

Likely failure modes to avoid:
Depicting AI as a standalone object rather than an evolving system
Using generic futuristic technology imagery without showing reuse or feedback
Suggesting unsupported physical infrastructure

Global factual restrictions:
Do not fabricate visible factual evidence. Do not imitate a named or living artist. Do not render interfaces or screenshots.
Do not render any words, letters, numbers, labels, captions, annotations, signage, diagrams with text, logos, watermarks, or pseudo-writing anywhere in the image.
If the concept cannot be represented without visible text, represent the relationship through shape, scale, spacing, direction, grouping, interruption, containment, or contrast instead.

visual_brief.alt_text: A connected evolving system shows reusable foundations supporting repeated feedback and improvement across an AI product.
visual_brief.composition: Show a cultivated path of connected stages, with a stable shared foundation beneath it and feedback returning from later stages to earlier ones.
visual_brief.editorial_idea: AI products become durable systems when reusable interfaces, shared contracts, and feedback loops help agents improve over time.
failure_modes: Depicting AI as a standalone object rather than an evolving system
Using generic futuristic technology imagery without showing reuse or feedback
literal_elements: A visible foundation beneath the pathways
Small markers showing repeated user feedback and system improvement
visual_brief.visual_relationship: Reusable product foundations support a cycle of use, feedback, and improvement rather than a single isolated feature.
